Identify three major geopolitical consequences of shifting towards renewable energy sources for oil-dependent countries. The three major geopolitical consequences of shifting towards renewable energy are as follows: 1. Economic instability: Countries that rely entirely on crude oil exports for their economy will face a sharp drop in revenue, which may cripple their domestic budgets and social welfare programs. 2. Decrease in geopolitical influence: The dominance of oil producing countries on the global stage will decrease as the world's attention for energy security shifts away from fossil fuels to new technologies and minerals. 3. Political unrest: The decrease in oil revenues may lead to increased anti-government protests and economic recession in these countries, which may increase the risk of political instability.

The invention of the printing press in the sixteenth century completely changed the way knowledge spread in Europe, challenging the centuries-old social stratification. Before printing, books were handwritten and were restricted to the aristocracy or clergy of the church, maintaining their monopoly on education and power. The arrival of the press reduced the cost of books and made them accessible to the general public. This resulted in the spread of literacy and the rise of the middle and upper classes, which began to question the ideological dominance of the communist church and the modern social order.

Completely disconnected from work: Have a clear policy of turning off laptops and not checking emails or messages once your shift is over. 3. Flexibility: Because the team is in different time zones, prioritize 'asynchronous' (not face-to-face) communication to get the job done. 4. Focus on health: Encourage regular breaks and time for physical activity. 5. Clear goals: Work success is measured not by hours, but by results and deadlines so that there is no pressure on the team.

The clock will be running at 7:24 p.m. Logic: There are a total of 12 hours from 8:00 a.m. to 8:00 p.m. In 12 hours, the clock will move back 12 times 3 = 36 minutes. Subtracting 36 minutes from 8:00 p.m. gives a time of 7:24.

The length of a rectangle is twice its width. It measures 36 meters. What is the length and width of the rectangle? Answer with step-by-step reasoning. The width is 6 meters and the length is 12 meters.

If 5 machines take 5 minutes to make 5 objects, how long will it take 100 machines to make 100 objects, assuming that each machine works at the same speed? Answer with step-by-step reasoning. 5 minutes. Logic: If 5 machines make 5 objects in 5 minutes, this means that one machine takes 5 minutes to make one object. So, if 100 machines work together, they will all make 100 objects at the same time, because each machine will finish its object in 5 minutes.

What is the sum of all even numbers from 2 to 50? Explain your logic step by step. 650. Even numbers up to 2, 4, 6, ..., 50 form a parallel series. Number of terms = 50 ÷ 2 = 25. Sum = (number of terms) × (first term + last term) ÷ 2 = 25 × (2 + 50) ÷ 2 = 25 × 26 = 650.

Write a summary in one sentence: A district in South India has started distributing solar-powered lanterns in households where electricity is not available. Each lantern gets charged during the day and lights up for about eight hours at night. School officials say that children in these households are now able to study after sunset and attendance in evening literacy classes has increased.

Summarize in one sentence: A cooperative of small farmers in a rural area has begun to share the same set of modern harvesting machines instead of buying separate machines by each family. By pooling their money and timing equipment for different fields, farmers have cut their personal costs by almost half. This arrangement has also enabled poor families who were not able to afford such machines alone to harvest their crops on time.

Summarize in one sentence: Public health workers have found that a simple mixture of clean water, salt and sugar can save the lives of children suffering from severe dehydration due to diarrhea. This solution, which can be prepared at home, helps the body absorb fluids much more effectively than water alone. Teaching mothers how to make it correctly has greatly reduced infant mortality in areas where access to hospitals is limited.

Write a summary in one sentence: A coastal city, which used to dump its household waste in open pits, has started a program to separate the waste as wet and dry at source. Now food waste is being converted into compost for local gardens, while plastics and metals are being collected for recycling. Within a year, the amount of waste sent to pits has decreased by more than sixty percent.

Summarize in one sentence: Researchers studying sleep have found that people who maintain a set bedtime even on weekends are more likely to fall asleep early and feel more refreshed when they wake up. The body's internal clock relies on regular signals of light and dark, and irregular routines can weaken these signals. Volunteers who kept their bedtime stable for a month reported better mood and greater concentration during the day.

Summarize in one sentence: A group of villagers revived a dry pond, which once supplied water to their fields. By clearing the sludge and creating small soil barriers to store rainwater, they replenished the pond during the monsoon. This raised the groundwater level and revived the surrounding wells, allowing farmers to grow a second crop in a year.

Write a summary in one sentence: A national program has started to train youths in villages to repair mobile phones and small electronic devices. The three-month course teaches basic circuits, equipment use and customer service. Many graduates have opened small repair shops in their own communities, providing a service that previously required long trips to the city. A training program teaching village youth to repair phones and electronics has enabled graduates to open shops locally, relieving residents of long commutes to the city.

Summarize in one sentence: Scientists have found that some species of fungi partner with tree roots and exchange nutrients through underground networks. Fungi help trees absorb water and minerals from the soil, while trees provide the sugars formed during photosynthesis to fungi. This cooperation enables forests to share resources and cope with drought and disease stress.

Write a summary in one sentence: A city hospital has introduced a messaging system that reminds patients of the timing of their medication and follow-up appointments. Many patients with chronic illnesses would forget to take a dose of medication or go for a check-up earlier, worsening their condition. The hospital reports that since the start of the reminders, significantly more patients are completing their scheduled treatment. Messages sent by the hospital for medication and appointments have helped many patients struggling with chronic illnesses to complete their treatment as scheduled.

The trees also act as barriers that reduce the noise of passing vehicles and help keep the surrounding air cool during the hot months. Officials say the greenery has made roadside resting spots more comfortable and improved air quality in nearby settlements. Planting trees on the side of highways has reduced dust and noise, cooled the air, and improved comfort and air quality for commuters and nearby residents.

Summarize in one sentence: A team of engineers has designed a small and inexpensive sensor that sends alerts to mobile phones when the water tank is empty. In areas where water comes in only a few times a week, families often waste water or miss supplies. Early users of this device say they are now able to manage their limited water supply much more carefully.

For many families, free meals are a big reason to send their children to school regularly. Teachers observed that students are more attentive in the afternoon and get sick less often. One study found that free and nutritious lunches at the school level improve children's attendance, concentration, health and academic performance, as well as lower dropout rates.

Summarize in one sentence: Beekeepers in an agricultural area have observed that keeping beehives near orchards increases the yield of fruit trees. As the bees come and go between the flowers to collect cumin, they carry pollen that fertilizes the flowers. Farmers who let the beekeepers into their land harvested more fruit, while the beekeepers received a steady source of honey.

A community library that lends books as well as tools, sewing machines and sporting goods is helping residents, especially low-income families, to share resources instead of buying less-used items.

Astronomers have detected a distant planet orbiting a star like our Sun. The planet is located at a distance from its star where the temperature on the surface can allow liquid water to exist. Although it is not yet possible to go there, scientists say that studying its atmosphere can reveal whether conditions favorable to life exist there.

Summarize in one sentence: A women's self-help group in a rural area has created a mutual fund by combining monthly savings. Members can borrow from this fund for starting small businesses or for emergency needs at low interest, which has gotten rid of them from lenders charging higher interest. In three years, many women have opened tailor shops and vegetable stalls, and the group's savings have steadily increased.

Summarize in one sentence: A new mobile application allows farmers to take a picture of a leaf of a diseased plant and get immediate suggestions for possible disease and treatment. The app works by comparing the image with a large collection of known plant diseases stored in the phone. Farmers who do not have easy access to agricultural experts can now quickly identify problems and protect their crops.

Summary in one sentence: Health officials have launched a campaign to vaccinate cattle against a disease that is spreading rapidly in livestock and can devastate the herd of farmers. Teams are going to villages to administer free vaccination and teaching farmers to identify the early symptoms of the disease. Since the campaign started, cases of disease have decreased and milk production in the region has become more stable.

Summary in one sentence: The Central Government of India launched a programme to distribute free solar-powered water pumps to small farmers in three drought affected states. These pumps extract groundwater at no fuel cost, allowing farmers to irrigate their crops even in the dry season. In the first year, the participating farmers recorded a 40 per cent increase in their crop yield compared to farmers using traditional diesel pumps.

Summarize in one sentence: Malaria cases in sub-Saharan Africa have fallen by 30 percent in the past decade, largely due to the widespread distribution of insecticide-treated mosquito nets. International health organizations distributed more than 50 million mosquito nets to families in high-risk areas. The greatest decrease in malaria mortality was recorded in children, with a 40 percent decrease in deaths in children under five years of age. Health officials attribute this to the regular use of mosquito nets and community education campaigns. The widespread distribution of insecticide-treated mosquito nets has helped reduce malaria cases by 30 percent in a decade in sub-Saharan Africa, which has seen the greatest decrease in mortality in children under five years of age.

As the glaciers retreat, the flow of rivers is expected to become uncertain, threatening both agricultural and urban water supplies. Researchers have warned that if greenhouse gas emissions are not significantly reduced, many small glaciers could disappear altogether by 2100. Since 1970, the Himalayan glaciers have lost nearly 40% of their ice, threatening the rapid flow of water supplies to 20 million South Asians.

Summarize in one sentence: A community library program in rural areas of Rajasthan has been providing free books and reading assistance to children from 150 villages since 2019. Volunteers visit schools every week to teach children to read and give them books to take home. A survey conducted three years later showed that the comprehension of children aged 8 to 12 improved by an average of two grade levels. The program has also increased regular school attendance by 18% in the participating villages.

Summary in one sentence: A consortium of engineering universities in South India has developed a low-cost water desalination unit powered entirely by solar energy. The unit, once installed, converts 1,000 litres of seawater per day into drinkable water without any fuel. Prototype tests conducted on the Tamil Nadu coast have shown that the unit can supply clean water to a village of 300 people at a lower cost than commercially bottled water. The team plans to release this design as open-source so that it can be replicated in other coastal areas around the world.

Write a summary in one sentence: A study conducted in Bangladesh, India and Nepal examined the effect of small-group micro-finance loans on women-dominated households. Women who received a $200 to $500 loan were significantly more likely to start or expand a small business within 18 months than those who did not receive a loan. The household income of borrowers increased by an average of 22 percent during the study period.

One of the main reasons for this is the overuse of antibiotics in human medicine and animal husbandry, which accelerates bacterial growth by applying constant selective pressure.

Pilot programs run in parts of Northeast India have found that students' school attendance has improved after gaining access to online learning materials.

From this location, the spacecraft can continuously observe the Sun without interruption. Its primary mission is to study the mechanisms behind the solar corona, solar wind and solar flares, which can disrupt satellite communications and power grids on Earth. India's Aditya-L1 spacecraft, which is installed in continuous view of the Sun at the Earth-Sun Lagrangian point, is designed to study solar corona activity and the causes of those flares that can disrupt Earth's communications infrastructure.

Summarize in one sentence: A large clinical study of 12,000 adults in six countries over ten years found that people who consumed more than 5 grams of salt per day had a 23% higher risk of developing high blood pressure than those who consumed less than 3 grams. The World Health Organization recommends consuming more than 5 grams of salt per day, yet surveys show that the average consumption in most countries is well above this limit.

Summarize the following information in one sentence: According to a study published by the International Centre for Integrated Mountain Development, glaciers in the Hindu Kush Himalayan region have been melting rapidly since the 1990s. The study, using four decades of satellite images, found that the amount of ice in the region has decreased by about 40% since 1980. Since these glaciers provide water to major rivers like the Indus, Ganges and Brahmaputra, their disappearance is threatening the drinking water supply for more than 20 million people living in low-lying plains.

The Coastal Monitoring Programme in the Lakshadweep group has recorded that 60% of coral reefs have been destroyed in the last two decades due to rising sea temperatures. Coral bleaching occurs when the water temperature rises a few degrees Celsius above the summer maximum level, causing corals to expel their nutrients and coloring algae. Without algae, corals starve and turn white and if temperatures remain high, they die. Reef loss has reduced the fish population in the area by an estimated 35%, affecting the livelihoods of local fishing communities.

Summarize the following passage in one sentence: India is one of the world's largest producers of barley, a drought-resistant grain grown in Maharashtra, Rajasthan and Karnataka. After decades of declining consumption due to widespread availability of rice and wheat through government distribution programmes, barley has regained importance due to its nutritional value and low water requirement. The United Nations in response to the Indian government's proposal declared 2023 as the 'International Year of Barley'.

Write a summary in one sentence: Oral rehydration therapy (ORS), which is a simple mixture of water, salt, and sugar, was developed in the 1960s in Dhaka, Bangladesh, after clinical trials demonstrated that it could effectively treat severe dehydration caused by hay fever and diarrhea. Prior to its widespread use, patients often needed to be given fluids through the veins, which had to be given to areas with limited resources. The World Health Organization estimates that oral rehydration therapy has saved more than 500 million medical lives since its inception.

India's National Solar Energy Programme, which was launched in 2010, had a target of installing 100 GW of solar capacity by 2022, which has been largely achieved. Rooftop solar panels and large-scale ground-mounted solar farms are now powering millions of homes and businesses across the country. Small solar-powered microgrids have delivered electricity to communities for the first time in remote villages without access to the national power grid. The cost of solar panels has fallen by more than 80% between 2010 and 2023, making the technology accessible even to low-income households.

Summarize the main argument in one sentence: Microfinance institutions provide small loans to individuals who do not have access to traditional banking services, and these typically target women from low-income communities. The Rural Bank of Bangladesh, founded in 1983 by economist Muhammad Yunus, formulated this model by lending small amounts to groups of women who collectively guaranteed repayment of each other. Studies in South Asia and sub-Saharan Africa have shown that microfinance recipients have higher rates of starting businesses and sending their children to school than non-recipients.

Summarize the following passage in one sentence: Smallpox was one of the deadliest diseases in human history, claiming an estimated 30 million lives in the twentieth century alone. India played a key role in the final phase of eradication, as its last major outbreak occurred in the state of Bihar in 1975. In 1980, the World Health Organization officially declared the eradication of smallpox, making it the only human disease to be eliminated through intentional vaccination.

Despite this protective role, India's mangrove cover has declined by about 40 per cent in the last four decades due to shrimp farming and coastal urban expansion.

Summarize in one sentence: India's Midday Meal Scheme, which was implemented nationally in 2001, provides free cooked meals to children enrolled in government primary and higher primary schools every school day. Summarize the key finding in one sentence: Microfinance programs initiated by rural banks in Bangladesh in the 1970s provide small loans without any guarantees to low-income borrowers, who are organized into groups that share social responsibility for repayment. Summarize in one sentence: In 2022, astronomers using the James Webb Space Telescope detected carbon dioxide in the atmosphere of the planet WASP-39b, located about 700 light-years from Earth. This discovery demonstrated that the telescope can accurately analyze the chemical composition of the atmospheres of distant planets, setting up a new observing instrument to study whether habitable conditions exist on distant planets. The Silk Road was a network of land and sea trade routes connecting China, Central Asia, Persia, and the Mediterranean world from about 200 BC to the 15th century AD. The four main causes of soil erosion in agricultural land are: 1. excessive use of chemicals and pesticides: this destroys the natural fertility of the soil and the activity of microbial nests. 2. deforestation: the removal of trees reduces the water storage capacity of the soil and leads to soil erosion. 3. irrigation: this leads to the loss of salinity in the soil and the continuation of crop rotations.

What is inflation and how does it affect the daily expenses of a typical middle-class family? Inflation refers to a general rise in the prices of goods and services, causing a decrease in the purchasing power of the currency. Explain the basic difference between a federal government and a unitary government. Also give a real-life example of both styles of government. In contrast, in a unitary government all powers are held by the central government and local governments operate only under the central government, as in the United Kingdom or France.

Explain to a curious twelve-year-old how a vaccine prepares our body's defense system to fight off disease. A vaccine is actually a 'hypothetical exercise' or training for our body's defense system. When our body sees this 'fake' attack, it immediately develops special weapons to fight it, called antibodies.

Summarize in one sentence: Scientists who monitor Himalayan glaciers say that the volume of ice in the region has decreased by about 40 percent since 1970. Without these algae, coral populations starve and turn white; if the temperatures remain high, they become extinct.

Summarize in one sentence: Oral rehydration therapy, a simple mixture of water, salt and sugar, was developed in the 1960s in Dhaka, Bangladesh, after clinical trials that proved it to be an effective treatment for severe dehydration caused by hemorrhoids and diarrheal diseases. The Constitution of India is the supreme legal document of India, which provides a comprehensive framework of rules, rights and duties necessary to run the government of the country. It officially came into effect on 26 January 1950. It is considered the basis of the government of India because it not only clarifies the powers and limits of the various organs of government - legislative, executive and judicial - but it also ensures fundamental rights to every citizen of the country. It is the Constitution that lays the foundation of democracy and gives supremacy to the rule of law.

Explain in detail what the Green Revolution was, which crops it focused on and how it changed food production in South Asia during the 1960s and 1970s. The Green Revolution was an agricultural policy initiated in the 1960s whose main objective was to massively increase grain production using modern technologies, fertilizers and advanced seeds. It focused mainly on wheat and rice crops.

In a hydroelectric dam, the kinetic energy of flowing water is used to turn the turbines. The turbine is connected to a generator, which converts this rotational speed into electrical energy.

The Panchayati Raj System is a three-tier system of rural local self-governance in India. It has three levels: 1. Gram Panchayat (at village level), 2. Panchayat Committee or Zone Panchayat (at block level), and 3. District Council (at district level). The main objective of this system is to decentralize democracy so that the rural people can solve their problems themselves and play an active role in the implementation of government schemes.

In rain-dependent areas, what four practical measures can small farmers take to protect their crops and maintain soil moisture during a sudden drought? Make a detailed list. The four main measures to cope with drought are: 1. Mulching (mulch): Cover the soil surface with dry grass, straw or leaves so that the sun does not fall directly on the soil and the evaporation of moisture is reduced. 2. Drip irrigation or drop-drop irrigation: Use drip techniques to use water directly in the roots of plants, so as not to waste water.

Inflation, which is called inflation in economics, refers to the general increase in the prices of goods and services in the market. When inflation rises, the purchasing power of the currency, i.e. the price of money, decreases. The main causes of inflation include an increase in demand (when there is less supply and more buyers), an increase in production costs (such as an increase in the price of raw materials or fuel), and an increase in the supply of money in the market.

The water cycle is the continuous process by which water passes from the surface of the earth to the atmosphere and then back to the earth as rain or ice. This process occurs in the phases of evaporation, condensation and precipitation. The water of the seas, rivers and lakes rises to the surface as steam from the heat of the sun, which is called evaporation.

Name five effective ways for a small farmer farming in areas with low and irregular rainfall to maintain soil moisture and preserve crops during drought. 1. Use mulching: Cover the top surface of the soil with dry grass, straw or leaves to prevent moisture from blowing from the sun. 2. Adopt drip irrigation: Use a drip irrigation system that delivers water directly to plant roots to prevent water wastage.

Bacteria are single-celled living organisms that can thrive outside or inside the body, whereas viruses are very small parasites that need a living cell to survive. This difference is extremely important for treatment because antibiotics are used to kill bacterial infections, but antibiotics have no effect on the virus. For diseases caused by viruses, doctors either give the body's immune system time to heal or use anti-viral drugs.

Summary in one sentence: A Fortified Rice Distribution Programme in Odisha included iron, folic acid and vitamin B12 in the rice supplied through the state's public food distribution system. Summarize the following passage in one sentence: The Indian Meteorological Department's Cyclone Early Warning System now issues alerts up to 120 hours before a storm hits the Bay of Bengal coast, whereas two decades ago the time limit was only 48 hours. This improvement relies on a dense network of Doppler radar stations, Ocean Boy and satellite data, which help to keep a more accurate eye on storm intensity and path. Since the implementation of this expanded warning system, coastal evacuations have become faster and more systematic, and cyclone-related deaths have decreased by more than 80% despite several severe storms. Scientists say the rest of the deaths are mainly occurring in remote areas where roads are less convenient.

A study of hospital-acquired infections in twelve major cities of India found that more than 70% of the bacteria tested were resistant to at least one common antibiotic. Excessive use and incomplete courses of antibiotics in both human medicine and animal husbandry have been identified as the main causes of this resistance. Researchers estimate that prolonged hospitalizations and second-line treatments due to drug-resistant infections cost India's health system Rs 30 billion annually. These findings have led to strict guidelines and demand for mandatory antibiotic management programs in public hospitals.

Summarize in one sentence: Tamil Nadu is ahead of any other state in India in electricity generation from wind power, with an installed capacity of over 10,000 MW, spread over the mountain passes where strong seasonal winds pass through. Grid operators attribute the diversity in renewable energy sources to reducing dependence on coal-fired plants during peak months of demand.

Summarize the following information in one sentence: Coral reefs spread over about 500 square kilometers in Lakshadweep Island suffered massive bleaching over two consecutive summers due to rising sea surface temperatures. Marine biologists recorded a 35% mortality rate in affected coral colonies after the second bleaching.

Write a summary in one sentence: The National Plant Genetic Resources Bureau of India maintains a seed repository, which stores over 430,000 specimens of crop varieties, wild plants and traditional varieties developed by farming communities of the subcontinent over centuries. This collection is considered vital for breeding new crop varieties capable of withstanding drought, new pest species and changing rainfall patterns. Access to the repository is available to research institutes and plant breeders working on food safety projects.

Summarize in one sentence: Researchers studying groundwater scarcity in Punjab and Haryana found that the water level in both states has declined by an average of 33 centimeters per year over the past two decades. This decline is mainly due to intensive paddy cultivation, which requires heavy amounts of irrigation water from tap wells during the summer season. Satellite data used in the study showed that the areas with the most severe decline coincide with those districts that adopted double crop systems after the Green Revolution. Scientists have warned that if drainage continues at the current rate, deep water irrigation may become economically unavailable in the next 25 years.

Write a summary in one sentence: Under a digital literacy initiative in rural areas of Karnataka, 1,50,000 women were trained in basic computer operation and internet use in two years, for which sessions were held at village-level general service centres. A follow-up survey found that 60% of the trained women recorded a significant increase in their household income within six months of completing the course.

Marine biologists have identified important breeding grounds, which protecting them could allow fish populations to re-grow within a decade. About 35 lakh families have benefited in the first year of the scheme. Blood tests conducted on children in the districts covered by the scheme have revealed a 25% reduction in anaemia after twelve months.

The Indian Meteorological Department's Cyclone Warning System now provides 120 hours of advance warning along the Bay of Bengal coast; two decades ago it was only 48 hours. Accurate monitoring using Doppler radar stations, sea buoys, and satellite information can predict cyclone intensity and trajectory. With the introduction of this system, the evacuation of people from coastal areas has been speeded up and the fatality rate has been reduced by more than 80 percent despite strong cyclones.

In a study conducted in hospitals in twelve major cities in India, more than 70% of the bacteria tested were found to be resistant to common antibiotics. The main reasons for this are overuse and undertreatment of antibiotics in humans and animals. The researchers estimate that the increase in hospital stays and treatment costs due to drug-resistant infections will cost the health sector of India an estimated Rs 3,000 crore annually.

The coral reefs, which cover an area of about 500 square kilometres in the Lakshadweep archipelago, have been subjected to extreme bleaching for two consecutive summers due to rising sea level temperatures. The phenomenon is caused by thermal stress that causes corals to release algae in their lakshas, which turns the reefs white and makes them prone to disease. Marine biologists have found that 35 percent of the affected corals have been destroyed since the second white event.

The National Bureau of Plant Genetic Resources in India maintains a seed bank that holds more than four lakh seeds, including crop varieties, weeds and traditional farming practices developed by agricultural communities over centuries. Scientists check the quality of the stored seeds at regular intervals and reproduce those with decreased germination capacity. The collection is projected to develop new crop varieties capable of surviving drought, new pests and changing rainfall patterns.

It is believed that fish stocks can be recovered within ten years if important breeding grounds identified by marine biologists are protected.

With the loss of languages, unique oral histories, knowledge of medicinal plants, and grammatical structures not found in other languages are lost forever. Although efforts are being made to document the linguistic knowledge of the last generation of elderly speakers in India, Papua New Guinea, and South America, researchers conclude that language loss is irreversibly affecting human cultural and intellectual diversity.

India's Mars Orbiter mission, launched in 2013, is the first Asian spacecraft to reach the orbit of Mars and the first in the world to achieve this feat on its first attempt. Completed at a cost of approximately US$74 million, the mission was the lowest-cost Mars mission at the time.

Underground rainwater storage systems built beneath public parks in Chennai collected more than 800 million litres of rainwater during a monsoon period and raised the city's decreased groundwater level. The system, which was connected to existing rainwater channels without the need for pumping, raised the groundwater level in the surrounding areas by an average of three metres in six months.

Rapid urbanization in Indian cities has reduced the amount of water-absorbing land by more than 60 percent over the past three decades, which has significantly increased the risk of lightning floods during the monsoon season. Concrete roads and buildings prevent rainwater from reaching the ground, causing it to push into drainage systems built for low population. Over the past fifteen years, large-scale floods in cities such as Mumbai, Chennai and Hyderabad have caused hundreds of billions of rupees worth of damage and hundreds of millions of deaths.

Overuse of antibiotics in humans and livestock has accelerated the growth of new types of resistant bacteria. Many state governments have initiated projects to restore tropical rainforests over hundreds of kilometers of coastal areas. This is delaying the implementation of sustainable farming and food conservation measures, but there is not yet enough information to prevent the imminent decrease in groundwater levels.

In a ten-year study of 15,000 adults in five countries, researchers found that walking at least 7,000 steps a day reduced the risk of death from heart disease by 50 to 70 percent. The researchers point out that this benefit is obtained regardless of age or walking style, and that the effect is consistent regardless of body weight and prior health problems. Based on these findings, health professionals prefer campaigns to encourage daily walking over gym training.

Reports indicate that about 163 million people in rural India still do not have access to safe drinking water and thousands of children under the age of five die in the country every year due to diarrheal diseases caused by sewage. The rapid expansion of metro rail networks in Indian cities has prompted daily commuters to switch from private vehicles to public transport. Free textbooks have been distributed to more than four crore students annually to provide relief to economically backward families.

Describe the primary duties of a city or town's local government (municipal corporation or town committee) and explain how their decisions affect the daily lives of ordinary citizens. The primary duties of a local government include managing city cleanup, building and repairing roads and streets, providing street lights, drainage systems, and maintaining parks and public spaces.

Photosynthesis is the biological process by which plants use sunlight, water, and carbon dioxide to produce their food (glucose). It requires three basic elements: (1) sunlight (which absorbs the chlorophyll in the leaves), (2) water (which is obtained from the soil by the roots), and (3) carbon dioxide (which is absorbed from the air by the leaf pores).

Write a brief explanation of what inflation is, its common causes, and how it affects the purchasing power of an average household. Inflation refers to a steady rise in the prices of goods and services, resulting in a decrease in the value of currency. Its primary causes include an increase in demand, an increase in the cost of production, or an increase in the government's supply of currency.

What is a census? Why do governments conduct it regularly? Give two examples of how census data is used to make decisions. A census is the process of counting all the people living in a country and gathering their socioeconomic details.

When blood enters the kidneys, millions of tiny filters, called nephrons, separate waste, excess salts, and urea from the blood. These filtered wastes, along with water, accumulate in the urine and are then excreted from the body. Drinking water improves blood flow and keeps the kidneys fluid to clean and flush out wastes.

Panchayat Raj is a tri-level system of local self-government in India that brings democracy to the bottom level in rural areas. It consists of a 'Gram Panchayat' at the lowest level, elected by the 'Gram Sabha' consisting of the village voters. The main functions of a Gram Panchayat include village sanitation, provision of clean drinking water, construction and repair of roads, maintenance of schools, and setting up agricultural villages.

Photosynthesis is the biological process by which plants prepare their food in the presence of sunlight. The main components of this process are carbon dioxide, water and sunlight, while the resulting release of glucose (nutrients) and oxygen.

Under a rice enrichment programme in Odisha, iron, folic acid and vitamin B12 were added to rice supplied through the public distribution system, benefiting about 35 lakh households in the first year. Blood tests of children in the participating districts showed a 25% reduction in anemia (anemia) rates after 12 months. Using these results from Odisha as a model, the central government is expanding the program to eleven more states.

Summarize the following paragraph in one sentence: The Indian Meteorological Department's Hurricane Early Warning System now issues warnings up to 120 hours before a storm hits the Bay of Bengal coast, compared to just 48 hours two decades ago. This improvement relies on an extensive network of Doppler radar stations, seabirds and satellite data that accurately track the storm's intensity and path.

Summarize the following text in one sentence: A study of hospital-acquired infections in twelve major cities in India found that more than 70% of the bacterial samples tested are resistant to at least one common antibiotic. Overuse of antibiotics in both human and veterinary medicine and failure to follow a course of medication have been identified as the main causes of this resistance. Researchers estimate that drug-resistant infections cost the Indian health system about Rs 30 billion annually during long hospital stays and secondary treatment.

Summarize the following sentence in one sentence: Tamil Nadu generates more electricity from wind power than any other state in India, with an installed capacity exceeding 10,000 MW spread across the hill slopes where strong seasonal winds blow. Wind farms in these areas take advantage of monsoon winds that are expected to increase electricity production from May to September. Grid operators believe that the diversified sources of renewable energy have reduced reliance on coal-fired power plants in the months of high electricity demand.

Summarize the following text in one sentence: Coral reefs covering about 500 square kilometers in the Lakshadweep Islands suffered massive bleaching over two consecutive summers due to rising sea level temperatures. Bleaching occurs when heat stress causes corals to shed algae in their tissues, rendering their colonies white and vulnerable to disease. Marine biologists recorded a 35 percent death rate in infected coral colonies after the second bleaching outbreak.

The National Bureau of Plant Genetic Resources of India maintains a seed repository containing more than 430,000 crop species, wild plants, and traditional farming specimens that have been produced by farmers across the subcontinent over the centuries. Scientists periodically test the fertility of these seeds and regrow those specimens that have become less viable to grow. This repository is considered critical for developing new crops that can withstand droughts, new pests, and changing weather conditions. Research institutions and plant breeders can access this repository for food safety projects.

Satellite data used in the study showed that the areas with the highest water scarcity correspond to the districts that adopted double cropping after the Green Revolution. Summarize the following text in one sentence: Under a digital literacy initiative in rural Karnataka, one hundred and fifty thousand women were trained in basic computer and internet usage in two years, whose sessions were held in village level shared centers. Participants learned how to get children into schools through access to government incentive portals, online small business transactions and digital registration. A survey found that sixty percent of trained women reported a significant increase in household income within six months of completing the course.

Summarize the following text in one sentence: Fish stocks in the Arabian Sea have declined by about 35% over the past two decades due to overfishing and the proliferation of industrial trawler fleets. Small-scale fishermen in the coastal areas of Kerala and Karnataka say their daily catch is less than half of what it was twenty years ago. Biologists have identified key breeding areas where fish populations could recover within a decade if protected.

Summarize the following paragraph in one sentence: Health workers in rural areas of Rajasthan launched a pilot program to store vaccines in solar-powered refrigerators in village health centres, replacing the old system of weekly delivery from district hospitals. These solar units kept the temperature stable even during prolonged power outages, reducing the rate of vaccine wastage from 18% to less than 2%.

To reach these conclusions, the researchers followed 15,000 children for eight years. In response, the governments of the three countries involved in the study announced plans to expand free preschool programs. An eight-year study of 15,000 children found that two or more years of preschool education significantly improved children's literacy and numeracy skills at age ten, benefiting most low-income children.

The developing countries of South and Southeast Asia are making a rapidly growing contribution to new projects. Analysts estimate that solar power will provide one-third of global electricity by 2040, if the current rate of installation is maintained. The price of solar power has fallen by 89% between 2010 and 2023, making it the world's cheapest source of energy, and analysts estimate that solar power will provide one-third of the world's electricity by 2040.

Summarize the following text in one sentence: Linguists estimate that by the end of this century, half of the world's nearly 7,000 languages will be extinct as new generations move toward national or globally dominant languages. When a language dies, it also carries with it a unique linguistic history, knowledge of medicinal plants, and grammatical structures not found in any other language. Community-led documentation projects have recorded thousands of hours of conversation by the last-speaking elders in India, Papua New Guinea, and South America.

Summarize the following paragraph in one sentence: Agricultural scientists say that global food security depends on a few crops, including rice, wheat, and corn, which provide more than 50 percent of the calories needed by the human population. Dependence on high-yielding varieties with similar genetic traits puts the food supply at risk from a single disease or insect infestation. Seed banks in Norway, India, and Peru are storing seeds of traditional varieties, while researchers are grafting ancient varieties with modern crops to produce higher yields as well as immunity to disease.

Summarize the text in one sentence: India's Mars Space Mission, launched in 2013, became the first Asian spacecraft to successfully enter Mars orbit and the first in the world to do so on its first attempt. The spacecraft cost about US$74 million, making it the lowest-cost Mars mission at the time. The instruments on board mapped the surface of Mars and measured the composition of its thin atmosphere. The mission proved that the Indian Space Research Organization can conduct deep space scientific research at a much lower cost than agencies in the United States and Europe.

Engineers connected these chambers to existing rain gutters so that water could automatically enter without pumping, significantly reducing construction costs. Within six months of the system's activation, the average groundwater level in neighborhoods rose by three meters. The city authorities have prevented the project from causing severe water shortages in the dry season for about 12 million residents.

Summarize the following paragraph in one sentence: Rapid urbanization in Indian cities over the past three decades has reduced water-absorbing land by more than 60 percent, increasing the risk of sudden flooding during the monsoon. Concrete roads and buildings prevent rainwater from being absorbed into the ground, causing it to flow into drainage systems designed for very small populations. Cities such as Mumbai, Chennai and Hyderabad have experienced major floods in the past 15 years, causing billions of rupees in financial losses and hundreds of quakes.

Summarize the following paragraph in one sentence: Kerala has maintained a literacy rate of over 96% for over two decades, the highest of all Indian states. The state achieved this success through early investment in public schools, an extensive network of community libraries, and evening literacy classes for adults who were deprived of formal education. A state-level literacy campaign launched in the 1980s trained local volunteers to teach reading and writing in villages.

Summarize the following paragraph in one sentence: Bangladesh has lost more than half of its coastal mangrove forests in the last fifty years due to shrimp farming and conversion of agricultural land. Sundarbans, the world's largest mangrove forest on the Bangladesh-India border, protects coastlines from cyclones. Government and local community programs have planted more than two million hectares of new mangroves along the Bay of Bengal coast since 2005.

In 2009, a failed Russian satellite collided with an active U.S. communications satellite, producing thousands more pieces that are still in orbit. Scientists warn that if active debris removal missions and stricter regulations for destroying satellites are not implemented, the density of the orbiting region may eventually become so high that it is dangerous to operate safely around the Earth.

Summarize the following paragraph in one sentence: Several major cities in India have begun to install meters on domestic water connections in order to reduce water wastage and raise funds for infrastructure improvements. Prior to the installation of meters, households had no incentive to fix water leaks or reduce water use due to flat-rate billing. Pilot programs run in three cities found that metered billing reduced average household water use by 22% within a year.

Summarize the following paragraph in one sentence: The government of Bangladesh introduced a scholarship program under which cash was given to families who enrolled their daughters in secondary school. Within ten years, the enrolment rate of girls in secondary school rose from 33 percent to more than 70 percent. Researchers found that girls who completed secondary school married late and had fewer children on average.

Overuse of antibiotics in human and veterinary medicine has accelerated the spread of resistant bacterial strains. Researchers warn that even minor surgical procedures can be fatal if untreatable infections are not regulated. International health organizations are coordinating global calls for monitoring of antibiotic use and action to fund new treatments.

Summarize the following paragraph in one sentence: Mangrove forests along India's coasts have declined by about 40% during the past five decades due to shrimp farming and urban development. Several state governments have initiated afforestation programs that have restored mangroves on hundreds of kilometers of coastline. Mangroves absorb wave energy as natural barriers and protect coastal villages from hurricanes.

Summarize the following text in one sentence: In 2024, India will make it mandatory to include iron, folic acid and vitamin B-12 in rice distributed under the government system. Nearly half of the women and children in the country suffer from iron-deficiency anemia (anemia), which affects their energy, mental capacity and ability to work. Adding nutrients to rice costs little per kilogram, but it provides essential nutrition to millions of people receiving government rations.

Summarize the following text in one sentence: Agricultural enterprises in South Asian countries are using satellite imagery to monitor crop health over millions of hectares, eliminating the need for field inspectors to visit every field. Multi-spectral sensors measure light reflected from plant leaves, allowing analysts to detect drought, pest infestation and nutrient deficiencies weeks before they are visible on the ground.

Summarize the following passage in one sentence: In the Indo-Gangetic plains, which span parts of India, Pakistan and Bangladesh, groundwater levels are falling rapidly due to severe irrigation for rice and wheat cultivation. According to data from NASA's GRACE gravity satellite, groundwater reserves in the region are declining at a rate of more than 50 cubic kilometers per year. The current rate of water withdrawal could cause the water reserves available in parts of Punjab and Haryana to run out within three decades, jeopardizing the food security of millions of people.

Summarize: A ten-year study of 15,000 adults in five countries found that those who walked at least 7,000 steps a day had a 50 to 70 percent lower risk of dying from heart disease than those who walked fewer than 3,000 steps. The benefit was the same across all age groups and regardless of whether the walk was completed in one go or at intervals throughout the day.

The disease slows children's mental development and affects their ability to concentrate in school. The governments of India, Bangladesh and Nepal have introduced iron-rich flour and school feeding programs to address the problem. Initial data from these programs suggest that daily iron intake can restore hemoglobin levels to healthy levels within three months.

Summarize the following text in one sentence: Mangrove forests in coastal areas of India protect about 400 million people from storms and floods. These ecosystems serve as breeding grounds for fish and crabs, providing livelihoods for millions of fishermen. Over the past three decades, nearly a quarter of India's mangrove area has been lost due to land acquisition and the spread of aquaculture.

Over 7,000 cold storage facilities have been built across the country in the last decade under central government-funded Cold Chain Infrastructure projects. Studies show that farmers who have access to cold storage get up to 30% more value for their produce than farmers who are forced to sell their crop immediately after harvest. Farmers who lose up to 20% of their annual food crop after harvest lose up to 30% of their food crop, and the government has compensated up to 7,000 farmers for the construction of more than 30% of their food storage facilities.

Summarize the following text in one sentence: India has achieved over 100 GW of solar power capacity in 2024, making it one of the fastest countries to reach this milestone. This growth was made possible thanks to falling prices of solar panels, government subsidies for rooftop installations, and large-scale solar parks in Rajasthan and Gujarat. Solar power now meets about 15% of India's total electricity demand during daytime hours. Analysts estimate that the country could reach 300 GW of solar power capacity by 2030 if the current trend of investment continues.

Summarize in one sentence the following text: The global population of snow leopards is estimated at less than 7,000, with a large portion found in the high mountain ranges of India, Nepal, and Bhutan. Between 2020 and 2025, the Camera Trap Survey recorded a 5% increase in their numbers in North India's monitored areas, due to strict anti-poaching measures and conservation programs by local conservationists. However, naturalists warn that climate change is changing their habitat so rapidly that prevention alone is not enough.

Summarize the following text in one sentence: About 163 million people in India, most of whom live in rural areas, still do not have access to safe drinking water. Contaminated water is a major cause of diarrheal diseases, killing thousands of children under five years of age in the country every year. The government's Jal Jeevan Mission, launched in 2019, aims to bring water through pipes to every rural household.

The rapid expansion of metro rail networks in Indian cities has shifted millions of daily commuters away from the use of private vehicles. The Delhi metro system currently carries more than six million passengers on weekdays and is credited with reducing carbon dioxide emissions from the city's atmosphere by about 390,000 tons annually. Similar expansions are underway in Bangalore, Hyderabad and Pune.

Summarize the following paragraph in one sentence: Antibiotic resistance is now recognized as one of the most serious threats to global public health, causing an estimated 1.3 million deaths each year from resistant infections. Overuse of antibiotics in human medicines and livestock farming accelerates the spread of resistant bacterial strains. The World Health Organization has urged countries to limit over-the-counter sales of antibiotics and reduce their protective use in agriculture.

Summarize the following paragraph in one sentence: Delhi recorded its worst air quality to date during the winter of 2023, with particulate matter levels more than ten times the safe limit for several consecutive days. Explain the difference between infectious and non-infectious diseases. Give two examples of both, and also explain what measures public health systems take to deal with both types of diseases. Infectious diseases are diseases that are spread from person to person through germs such as bacteria or viruses, such as tuberculosis (TB) and influenza. In contrast, non-communicable diseases do not spread from person to person and are often caused by lifestyle, genetic or environmental factors, such as diabetes and high blood pressure. Public health systems provide vaccination campaigns, hygiene awareness and quarantine measures to prevent infectious diseases.

What is photosynthesis and why is it necessary for life on earth? Explain it in simple language understandable to schoolchildren. Explain the difference between communicable and non-communicable diseases, give two examples of both, and describe what measures the public health system takes to deal with both types. Communicable diseases are those that are spread from person to person by germs, viruses, or bacteria, such as the common cold or measles. In contrast, non-communicable diseases are not transmitted from person to person and are usually caused by lifestyle, genetics, or environmental factors, such as diabetes or heart disease.

What is the right to education, why do governments include it as a fundamental right, and what are the responsibilities of schools and parents under this right? Write a brief explanation. The right to education is the basic need of every child to have access to quality and free education. Governments give it the status of a fundamental right in order to eliminate ignorance in society, promote economic growth, and give everyone the opportunity to express their potential.

What is the process of photosynthesis? Explain the role of sunlight, water and carbon dioxide in this process. Photosynthesis is the biological process by which plants use sunlight as energy to produce their food, glucose, from water and carbon dioxide. In this process, sunlight is the primary source of energy that initiates a chemical reaction.

The five main benefits of crop rotation are as follows: (1) It increases soil fertility because different crops take in different nutrients from the soil; (2) It reduces the spread of pests and diseases because specific insects breed on the same crop; (3) It contributes to the destruction of weeds because crop rotation breaks their natural cycle; (4) It maintains the structure and moisture of the soil, reducing soil erosion; and (5) It stabilizes a farmer's income because crop rotation reduces the risk of market volatility.

What is a census, why do governments conduct it, and how is the information obtained from it used in public planning? Write a brief paragraph on this. A census is a process by which a country's total population is regularly counted and data on their socioeconomic status is collected. Governments conduct this process in order to know how many people live in the country, what their needs are, and how resources should be allocated.

Explain the concept of 'inflation' or inflation in economics: What causes it, what effects it has on the purchasing power of the average person, and what measures governments generally take to control it? Inflation refers to a steady rise in the prices of goods and services, which in turn lowers the value of currency. Its main causes include abnormal increases in demand, rising production costs (such as the cost of raw materials or fuel), and an excess of the amount of currency in circulation.

Rs 13,310 (after the first year: Rs 10,000 × 1.10 = Rs 11,000; after the second year: Rs 11,000 × 1.10 = Rs 12,100; after the third year: Rs 12,100 × 1.10 = Rs 13,310; similarly, the total amount = Rs 10,000 × (1.10) 3 = Rs 10,000 × 1,331 = Rs 13,310).

Summarize the following text in one sentence: India's Unified Payments Interface, commonly referred to as UPI, completed more than 13 billion transactions in a single month in 2024, making it one of the world's largest real-time digital payment systems. The platform allows users to instantly transfer money between bank accounts using mobile phones without the need for credit cards or cash. Since its launch in 2016, UPI has more than 30 crore active users nationwide. Several other countries including Singapore, France and the United Arab Emirates have started accepting UPI payments for international transactions.

Summarize the following sentence: TB is the leading cause of death from infectious diseases worldwide, claiming an estimated 1.3 million lives each year. South Asia bears a disproportionately large burden of the disease, with India, Pakistan, and Bangladesh together accounting for about 45 percent of the world's new cases. Drug-resistant strains of the bacterium have become a growing concern because of their long and costly treatment cycles.

Summarize the following statement in one sentence: About one-third of the food produced for human consumption worldwide is wasted, amounting to about one-tenth of three billion tons annually. Most of the waste in rich countries occurs at the consumer level, where homes and restaurants throw away edible food, while in low-income countries the losses are primarily due to inadequate post-harvest storage and poor transportation systems.

The disaster killed more than 400 people and displaced about 14 lakh people to government relief camps. Subsequent government reviews revealed that unconstruction in flooded areas and simultaneous release of water from multiple dams exacerbated the disaster. Kerala has since introduced strict land use regulations and coordinated dam management mechanisms to reduce future flood risks.

Scientists warn that if the density of debris in orbit increases too much, the collision could trigger an automatic chain reaction that would render those orbits permanently unusable. Little progress has been made in international negotiations on rules for removing debris and disabling satellites.

Summarize the following paragraph in one sentence: Mangrove forests cover an area of about 150,000 square kilometers along tropical and subtropical coasts and their dense roots protect beaches from storm surges and erosion. These forests are capable of absorbing four times more carbon than normal terrestrial forests, making their protection critical to mitigating the effects of climate change. Since 1980, commercial shrimp farming, urban development, and logging have wiped out more than 35% of mangrove forests worldwide.

Summarize the following sentence: Researchers studying urban heating have found that urban centers in South Asia are on average 3 to 5 degrees Celsius hotter than their surrounding rural areas, a phenomenon known as the 'urban heat island' effect. Dense construction, dark road surfaces, and the use of concrete in place of vegetation traps heat by reducing the ability to release moisture. During extreme heat, this extra heating poses serious health risks, especially for workers working outdoors, the elderly, and residents without access to cooling.

Summarize the following text in one sentence: Antimicrobial resistance occurs when bacteria, viruses, or fungi adapt to drugs that previously killed them, rendering conventional treatments ineffective. The World Health Organization calls it one of the greatest threats to global health and estimates that drug-resistant infections are already causing more than 12 million deaths annually. Overuse of antibiotics in human medicine and their widespread use in pharmaceuticals accelerate the process of resistance.

Summarize the following quote in one sentence: Chennai, located on the southeastern coast of India, was hit by severe flooding in 2015 when more than 1,800 mm of rain fell in a single month, almost double the city's annual average. The floods displaced more than 1.8 million residents and caused economic damage of US$3 billion. Investigations found that decades of unregulated construction had closed natural drainage routes and reduced the capacity of the land to irrigate.

Summarize the following text in one sentence: Over the past fifty years, worldwide literacy rates have steadily increased, with the proportion of literate adults rising from about 56% in 1970 to 87% in 2022. Sub-Saharan Africa and South Asia have seen the greatest improvement due to primary school enrollment and government campaigns. Despite this progress, some 77 million adults worldwide are still unable to read and write, two-thirds of whom are women. Researchers attribute this persistent illiteracy to limited access to schools, poverty, and early marriage.

Summarize the following text in one sentence: A study of sixteen major rivers in Asia found microplastic particles in each water sample, with the highest concentrations near densely populated urban areas. Plastic particles smaller than five millimeters enter rivers through wastewater discharges, stormwater runoff, and mismanagement of garbage. Fish and other aquatic animals eat these particles, which can accumulate in their bodies and reach the human food chain.

Summarize the following passage in one sentence: India has one of the world's largest documented repository of medicinal plants, with over 8,000 species used in traditional medical systems such as Ayurveda and Siddha. Many active compounds derived from these plants have been validated by pharmaceutical research and included in approved medicines for treating everything from inflammation to bacterial infections. However, over-exploitation and loss of natural habitat have put at least 350 species of medicinal plants at risk.

Summarize the following text in one sentence: According to data from the International Renewable Energy Agency, 1.37 million people were employed in the global renewable energy sector in 2022, an increase of almost one million from the previous year. The manufacturing and installation of solar photovoltaics accounted for the largest number of jobs, followed by liquid biofuels and wind energy. China, Brazil, the United States and India together account for nearly 70% of the world's total renewable energy jobs.

Summarize in one sentence: Antimicrobial resistance occurs when bacteria, viruses, or fungi develop ways to help them survive against drugs that kill them, rendering standard treatments ineffective. A 2022 study found that 1.27 million deaths were directly caused by drug-resistant bacterial infections and estimated that a total of five million deaths this year were attributed to this problem.

Summarize the following text in one sentence: India's Mid-Day Meal Scheme, one of the world's largest school feeding programmes, provides free cooked lunches to about 12 crore children attending government primary and upper primary schools every day. Studies reviewing the scheme have shown that it has increased school enrollment rates by up to 25% in some districts, especially among girls and children from low-income households.

Summarize the following passage in one sentence: Groundwater levels in peninsular India have declined sharply over the past two decades as agricultural irrigation accounts for about 89% of total water use in several states. Satellite data show that water reserves in parts of Gujarat, Rajasthan and Andhra Pradesh are decreasing by up to one centimeter per year, causing several shallow wells to dry up between April and June.

Summarize the following text in one sentence: India's National Sanitation Programme, which was launched to eradicate open defecation, built more than 10 crore domestic toilets in rural areas between 2014 and 2019. Government surveys show that the rate of open defecation in the rural population fell from less than 39 per cent in 2014 to less than 1 per cent by 2020. Health researchers recorded a significant decrease in the rate of diarrheal diseases among children under five years of age in districts with high access to toilets. Authorities have since expanded the programme to improve solid waste management and sewage sanitation in towns.

The Great Barrier Reef experienced its most extensive mass bleaching on record in 2022, affecting more than 90 percent of the reefs surveyed. Scientists say that if the global average temperature rises to 2 degrees Celsius from pre-industrial levels, most tropical coral reefs will experience a permanent rise in global temperatures. The Great Barrier Reef experiences its most extensive mass bleaching on record in 2022, affecting more than 90 percent of the reefs surveyed.

Summarize the following text in one sentence: India's Chandrayaan-3 spacecraft successfully landed near the south pole of the Moon in August 2023, making India the fourth country to soft-land on the Moon. The mission sent a rover named Parganj which travelled about 100 meters to the lunar surface and confirmed the presence of sulfur, iron and other elements in the soil. Data from the lander's instruments also provided evidence of water in the form of ice in permanent shadowy craters near the pole.

Summarize the following passage in one sentence: Microfinance institutions provide small loans to low-income individuals, especially women, who do not have access to traditional banking facilities. In Bangladesh, the Grameen Bank model laid the foundation for group loans, in which group members guarantee each other's loans, resulting in a repayment rate of more than 95%. Research reviewing lenders for ten years found that households who continued to access microloans on a regular basis had significantly increased incomes and spent more on children's education and healthcare.

Scientists have found that the eyes of these birds contain specific proteins called 'cryptochromes', which are sensitive to the Earth's magnetic field. When light strikes these proteins, a chemical reaction forms pairs of electrons whose 'spin' state is affected by geomagnetic field lines, allowing the birds to see the magnetic response as a kind of visual signal. These bio-directional devices work even on cloudy nights when stars are not visible. Laboratory experiments in which this magnetic perception was disrupted have confirmed that affected birds lose their directional accuracy.

In 1977, marine scientists discovered rich communities of organisms around hydrothermal vents, about 2,500 meters below the surface of the Pacific Ocean, using a small deep-sea submarine. Unlike almost all other known ecosystems, these communities do not rely on sunlight for energy. Instead, chemically synthesized bacteria convert hydrogen sulfide from the vents into organic matter, which forms the basis of a food chain and supports organisms such as tube worms, clams, and shrimps.

All elements heavier than hydrogen and helium - including carbon, oxygen, iron, and gold - were created within stars by a process called 'stellar nucleosynthesis', in which nuclear fusion reactions combine light atomic nuclei to form heavy nuclei. When giant stars run out of fuel, they explode as supernovae and emit these newly created elements into the surrounding space. Over billions of years, this material has subsequently joined the gases that make up stars and planetary systems.

India's Goods and Services Tax, implemented in July 2017, replaces more than a dozen Central and State indirect taxes with a single tiered structure. A positive integer is called a 'perfect number' if it is equal to the sum of all its proper divisors (all positive divisors except itself). The first two perfect numbers are 6 (because 1+2+3=6) and 28 (because 1+2+4+7+14=28). The third perfect number is 496. Find the sum of all its divisors, including itself, of 496, without listing all the individual divisors of 496.

In 1977, oceanographers using a deep-sea submarine discovered thriving colonies of organisms around hydrothermal vents at the bottom of the Pacific Ocean, about 2,500 meters below the surface. Unlike almost all other known ecosystems, these colonies do not rely on sunlight for energy. Instead, chemosynthetic bacteria convert hydrogen sulfide emitted from the vents into organic matter, forming the basis of a food chain on which tube worms, clams, and shrimp live.

When giant stars run out of fuel, they explode as supernovae and scatter these newly formed elements into the surrounding space. Over the course of billions of years, this material is incorporated into clouds of gas that are transformed into new star and planetary systems. Calcium in the bones and iron in the blood were present within these stars long before the formation of the solar system, about 4.6 billion years ago.

Under the soil of most forests, there is an extensive network of fungal fibers called mycorrhizae, which connect to the roots of about 90 percent of land-dwelling plant species. These fungi greatly increase the root area of host plants, helping trees absorb nutrients such as water and phosphorus that the roots alone cannot easily reach. In turn, the plants provide the sugar (sugar) fungi produced by photosynthesis.

Bioluminescence is found in many species, including some bacteria, fungi, jellyfish, and deep-sea fish. This light is produced by a chemical process in which a molecule called luciferin oxidizes in the presence of an enzyme called luciferase and releases energy in the form of light with very little heat. In the deep sea, where sunlight does not reach, approximately three-quarters of animals are bioluminescent, using light to attract prey, find mates, or isolate insects.

Krishnadevaraya ruled the Vijayanagara Empire in South India from 1509 to 1529 and is considered one of the greatest rulers of the Medieval Deccan. During his reign the empire reached its peak, covering large parts of present-day Karnataka, Andhra Pradesh and Tamil Nadu. He was also a noted poet of the Telugu language, who composed the literary masterpiece 'Amukta Malida'. His court attracted scholars, poets and artists from across the subcontinent, making the capital a centre of humble learning. He also established diplomatic and commercial relations with the recently arrived Portuguese crown on the western coast of India.

The Great Fire of London began in September 1666 at a bakery in Pudding Lane and spread rapidly to nearby wooden buildings over the course of four days. It destroyed about 13,200 houses, 87 churches and several civic buildings in an area of about 1.6 square kilometres. Despite the scale of the disaster, the number of recorded fatalities was surprisingly low.

In 1905, the British colonial administration under Viceroy Lord Curzon divided the Bengal Presidency into two separate provinces: East Bengal with Assam, and West Bengal. This was ostensibly due to administrative convenience, as Bengal was the most populous province of British India, but many Indians believed that the partition was intended to weaken Bengali nationalism by dividing the area along religious lines, with Muslims in the east and majority in the west. This partition gave rise to the Swadeshi movement, in which Indians boycotted British-made items and promoted alternative Indian-made items.

Historians often present her death as a symbol of the decline of classical sciences in the ancient Mediterranean world. Hypatia, a fifth-century Alexandria philosopher and mathematician, was one of the most prominent female scholars of classical antiquity, murdered by a mob in 415 CE; her death is often portrayed as a symbol of the decline of classical sciences in the ancient Mediterranean world.

The Panama Canal, which connects the Atlantic and Pacific Oceans through the Isthmus of Panama, was built by the United States and opened to shipping in August 1914 after nearly ten years of construction. The project required the removal of about 17 million cubic meters of soil and presented extremely difficult engineering challenges. An initial attempt by France in the 1880s failed because severe outbreaks of malaria and yellow fever killed thousands of workers.

This seasonal reversal provides about 80% of India's annual rainfall between June and September. Controlled slow cooling, called annealing, reduces the internal pressure that would otherwise cause the glass to shatter. The result is a solid, transparent sheet of glass that can be molded into a hard, brittle shape, which can be shaped like a hard shell, with lenses or lenses.

When a user initiates a payment through a mobile application such as UPI, the app generates an encrypted request that includes the payer's account details and the amount of the transaction. This request reaches a payment network, which sends it to the payer's bank for verification and authorization. The bank verifies the credentials of the account and makes sure that there are enough funds, then sends an approval signal back through the network.

The municipal water treatment plant purifies the raw water from rivers or reservoirs by passing through various stages before sending it to domestic pipes. First, chemicals such as lime are mixed so that fine suspended particles join together to form heavy clumps called 'fluxes', a process called coagulation (the process of distillation). The water then goes to settling tanks where these clumps settle below the distillation process. The purified water passes through layers of sand and gravel that polish the remaining fine particles and germs.

Microfinance institutions in South Asia provide small loans of between five hundred and five thousand dollars to low-income borrowers who do not have access to formal banking. In India, self-help groups deliver these loans mainly to rural women, who use this capital to start or expand small businesses such as tailoring, cattle raising, and food processing. Investigations reviewing lenders for five years show that households with access to microfinance have increased their incomes and are less dependent on informal borrowers who charge more than 50 percent interest annually.

The Goods and Services Tax (GST), introduced in July 2017 in India, replaced dozens of central and state indirect taxes with a single hierarchical structure. The reforms aimed to eliminate the impact of taxes imposed at different stages of production, increasing the value of manufactured goods by an estimated two to four percent. In the first two years of implementation, tax registration in small businesses increased by almost fifty percent as informal traders joined the formal economy to obtain input tax credits.

In 2023, India received about $125 billion in remittances, making it the world's largest recipient by absolute volume. A large portion of these remittances are spent on household expenses, school fees and medical expenses rather than productive investment, which limits their long-term impact on local economies. Economists say that remittance flows remain stable or increase during times of foreign economic stress, which gives them a counter-cyclical role, distinguishing them from foreign direct investment.

India's Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act, enacted in 2005, gives every rural household a legal right to employment based on a wage of one hundred days per year on public projects. The program is demand-driven: any adult who presents himself at a government workplace must be employed within fifteen days or be paid unemployment allowance. Independent reviews have shown that the scheme has reduced rural poverty rates in participating districts and reduced the wage gap between men and women by setting an equal minimum daily wage.

In cities such as Mumbai, Dhaka and Karachi, thirty to sixty percent of the urban population lives in informal settlements where land ownership is insecure, sanitation is inadequate and electricity supply is uncertain. Municipal governments are unable to collect enough property taxes to cover the costs of building roads, water systems and transport networks needed for regular urban expansion. This creates a cycle in which poor infrastructure becomes a barrier to regular infrastructure development.
